label: Thriller Bark moving ship-island system

anchor.name: Thriller Bark
anchor.debut_audit: The hand-authored local record uses the arc opening at chapter 442; chapter 443 is the verified visual gate for the giant gate and moving ship/island enclosing the Sunny.

atlas_anchor.warning: Thriller Bark is a moving ship. The atlas point is an authored voyage-stage pin, not a canon coordinate, bearing, or persistent location.

topology.coordinate_system: ship-local normalized theatre; Blender Z-up; +Y chosen only as authoring-forward
verified_relationships: Thriller Bark is a moving pirate ship built from a former West Blue island, not a fixed island.
A massive outer hull/wall and giant mouth gate enclose arriving vessels and the carried island terrain.
The carried terrain includes shore, dead forest, an older inner wall/moat system, graveyard, village, and Hogback mansion.
The main mast, giant sail, mast mansion, and four chains tying the outer wall toward the mast establish the ship-scale silhouette.
The Log Pose does not respond to Thriller Bark, reinforcing that the runtime should not treat it as a fixed magnetic island waypoint.
interpolated_relationships: Hull diameter, wall height, gate size, shoreline, inner moat, road network, mansion footprint, chain catenaries, and mast proportions are visual interpolation.
The nested sequence outer gate to shore/forest to graveyard/mansion to mast is preserved without claiming exact compass bearings.
The atlas anchor selects the story theatre only; ship heading and position must never be inferred from it.
topology.transformation_policy: The root is mobile and may drift as a whole. The chapter-443 mouth gate may close as a one-shot state change. Late bridge and hull destruction remain withheld until exact gates and visible geometry are verified.

chapter_logic.default_rule: Render only verified components whose gate has been reached. Move the entire root as a vessel; never resolve it as fixed world geography.
chapter_logic.unknown_gate_rule: Any bridge, collapse, hull breach, late-arc damage, or interior room without a verified first visual chapter remains default_hidden.

proposed_local_scene_layout.root: TB_SHIP_ROOT at local origin; all geographic modules parented to the moving vessel root

zones.purpose: chapter-443 enclosing silhouette and gate state
zones.placement: circular readable perimeter; exact diameter withheld
inside hull wall with a navigable approach corridor
nested around the central built zone; exact footprint interpolated
three readable masses around a central approach; bearings withheld
dominant rear/central vertical landmark in authoring composition, not a canon bearing

proposed_local_scene_layout.camera_read: Fog reveals the mouth gate and enclosing wall first; the camera then enters the island deck before pulling back at chapter 449 to prove the whole structure is a ship.

unreal_runtime_reuse.unreal_notes: Build all modules under one movable actor or level instance. Gate collision should follow the mouth-gate state. Fog belongs to the ship-local theatre. Do not use World Partition as if this were permanent terrain.
unreal_runtime_reuse.map_runtime_notes: Use an authored story-entry transform and optional drift animation. Never snap the vessel back to the atlas pin as a geographic truth. Runtime registration must identify entity_type as mobile ship-island.
unreal_runtime_reuse.lod_policy: Preserve outer wall/gate, mast/sail, mansion, and chain silhouette; collapse village, grave markers, trees, and tower detail first.

coordinate_policy.warning: No fixed coordinate, heading, hull dimensions, or internal bearings are canonized by this contract.

integration_contract.node_gate_policy: Read component_id, reveal_chapter, chapter_to_verify, verification, default_hidden, and ship_local_parent from glTF extras.
integration_contract.mobility_policy: Load at story-scene transform; any drift applies to TB_SHIP_ROOT and all descendants.

unresolved: Exact outer hull diameter, wall height, gate dimensions, deck/island coastline, moat geometry, routes, and building footprints remain interpolation.
No fixed atlas coordinate, heading, speed, or route bearing exists for the moving vessel; the local pin is only a story-stage anchor.
The exact first visual gate and external geometry for Wonder Garden bridge require panel-level verification, so the bridge is withheld.
The exact chapters and geometry for bridge destruction, hull breaches, and other late-arc damage are unresolved and remain hidden.
Interior mansion/mast room topology and exact chain attachment geometry are outside this contract.
